title: Erdős Conjecture on $ex(n;\{C_3,C_4\})$
context_text: Erdős conjectured this asymptotic formula for the maximum number of edges in an $n$-vertex graph of girth at least 5. The conjecture is still open; the best known asymptotic bounds give $\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}\leqslant\limsup_{n\to\infty}\frac{ex(n;\{C_{3},C_{4}\})}{n\sqrt{n}}\leqslant\frac{1}{2}$, due to Garnick, Kwong and Lazebnik.
paper_contribution: Improves lower bounds on $ex(n;\{C_{3},C_{4}\})$ for all $n\in\{74,75,\dots,198\}$ (except $n=96,97$) via a new hill-climbing propagation algorithm, strengthening the computational evidence for the lower bound side of the conjecture.
paper_title: Improved lower bounds on the maximum size of graphs with girth 5
